stance: Risk-aware quality tilt: rotate toward liquid, rising-revision cyclicals and quality compounders; cut the redundant Vedanta cluster and falling-revision laggards; bring beta back toward 1.
experience_note: Pre-registered watch items: (1) Because momentum is compressed this round, I am deliberately ARM-led — if over the next 1-3 months the high-ARM adds (ONGC, NMDC, GUJGASLTD, GRASIM, COFORGE, GLAND) do NOT outperform the falling-ARM names I exited, that is direct evidence the revision tilt is not paying here and I should de-weight ARM next round. (2) I collapsed the Vedanta complex on a diversification/illiquidity argument, not a price view — if the exited sub-entities (VAML/VOGL/VEDPOWER/VISL) sharply outrun VEDL, my "they are one bet" framing was wrong. (3) I trimmed beta toward 1; if the market rips and I lag purely on beta, that is an accepted, pre-registered cost of de-risking, not a stock-selection miss. What would change my mind: a clear momentum regime re-asserting (z_mom dispersion widening) would pull me back toward the momentum leg and away from pure revision-chasing.
